response.justification: The candidate has broad software development experience but is fundamentally mismatched for this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, there is zero evidence of Japanese language proficiency — a stated native-level requirement — and the candidate's language section lists only English, Romanian, and German. Beyond the language disqualifier, the resume is overwhelmingly web and cloud focused (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB, React), with only a brief 6-month stint involving C/C++ at National Instruments. There is no demonstrated experience in embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, security protocols such as MACsec or 802.1X, Linux kernel modification, or any layer-2/layer-3 networking domain knowledge.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, and embedded contexts
Some low-level C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and Linux listed as a skill
Demonstrated ability to work across full stack and pick up new technologies
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — this alone disqualifies the candidate from a hard requirement
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, etc.)
Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/cloud; C programming is minimal and not UNIX/embedded-focused
